summary: Pacing ring: adaptive daily baseline based on rolling 7-day average
description: Current pacing ring uses a fixed threshold. Frank Schultz (occupational therapist) reported that clients with fibromyalgia have variable daily capacity. Implement rolling 7-day average baseline with override option.

summary: NRS scale labels: add mid-range verbal descriptors for accessibility
description: Frank Schultz flagged via Intercom conv-finth-001: add 'mild' (2-3), 'moderate' (4-6), 'severe' (7-8) verbal anchors to the 0-10 NRS in the pacing ring. References Farrar et al. 2001 MCID threshold.

summary: Reader onboarding: occupation-based activity history intake
description: Frank Schultz recommendation: replace symptom-first intake with occupation-based intake. Ask 'what activities matter to you?' before 'what is your pain level?' Aligns with Occupational Therapy philosophy and improves engagement.

summary: Pacing ring animation causes excessive battery drain on older iOS devices
description: Reported by Cohort Participants. Pacing ring continuous animation uses 15% battery per hour on iPhone 11 or older. Optimize animation to use CSS transitions instead of JS canvas.

summary: Symptom log: add 'occupation impact' field alongside pain rating
description: Frank Schultz Q3 advisory input: symptom log should capture 'what activity were you doing?' and 'how much did this affect your ability to complete it?' alongside NRS rating. Grounds pain data in occupational context.

summary: Occupational Therapy dashboard: show participant's top 3 occupational goals on workload card
description: Frank Schultz advisory: therapist-facing workload card should surface the person's stated occupational goals (cooking, climbing, work), not just pain scores. This keeps the Occupational Therapy's professional reasoning occupation-centered.

summary: Professional note template: auto-populate SOAP format from pacing log data
description: Frank Schultz feature request: allow Occupational Therapy to auto-generate a resource note draft from the participant's pacing log (Subjective: pain trend, Objective: pacing ring adherence, Assessment: goal progress, Plan: suggested pacing adjustment).

summary: Bitterroot Rehab pilot program: finalize agreement and onboarding plan
description: Dr. Lisa Tran at Bitterroot Rehab has expressed interest in a professional pilot. Frank Schultz to coordinate. Target 3-5 Occupational Therapy clinicians and 10-15 participants. Frank is point of contact.

summary: Prepare Q3 peer consultation board agenda
description: Frank Schultz to prepare professional notes for July 14 advisory board call. Items: (1) participant onboarding, (2) partner dashboard v2, (3) Occupational Therapy integration API. Due June 30.

summary: MTOTA conference sponsorship and booth setup - August 2026
description: Finthesiss.ai sponsoring MTOTA symposium in August. Frank Schultz to present 15-min walkthrough. Coordinate with Rowan McAllister at MTOTA. Frank is primary contact.

summary: Flare-up mode: temporary lowered baseline with one-tap activation
description: Frank Schultz advisory: during a symptom flare, clients should be able to drop their pacing baseline temporarily without it being read as a permanent regression. One-tap 'flare day' toggle that auto-reverts after 72 hours unless extended.

summary: Rolling 7-day baseline resets to zero after timezone change
description: Reported by Cohort Participants traveling across zones. The rolling average in cohort action item recomputes from zero when the device timezone changes, wiping recent history from the ring. Anchor calculation to UTC day boundaries.

summary: Export pacing log as PDF for coach sharing
description: Frank Schultz request: let clients export a 30-day pacing summary as a PDF they can hand to their coach or trainer. Include the ring trend, NRS averages, and occupation-impact notes. No private data beyond what the person chooses to export.

summary: Onboarding: add cooking and climbing to activity picker presets
description: Following cohort action item, the occupation-based intake needs richer activity presets. Frank Schultz suggested common high-value activities (cooking, gardening, climbing, hiking, lifting children) so clients aren't forced into free text on day one.

summary: workload card: flag clients with no log entry in 7 days
description: Frank Schultz advisory: the Occupational Therapy dashboard should surface clients who have gone quiet (no pacing log in 7+ days) so the coach can reach out. Sort the workload by engagement risk, not alphabetically.

summary: resource note draft includes raw NRS array instead of summary
description: Follow-up to CLINDASH-51 prototype. The auto-generated SOAP draft is dumping the full NRS array into the Objective section instead of a 7-day average and trend. Summarize before insertion.

summary: Bitterroot pilot: draft de-identified data handling addendum
description: Follow-up to OPS-12. Dr. Lisa Tran requested a written addendum on how pilot data is de-identified before it reaches MCWC Chronic Pain Program. Frank Schultz to draft with legal review. No participant-identifying fields leave the clinic tenant.

summary: Schedule July advisory board Zoom and send calendar holds
description: Frank Schultz to set up the July 14 advisory board Zoom (1 hour, 1pm MT) and send holds to Sasha, Willa, and Carla. Coordinate around Frank's protected clinic block.

summary: Draft coach-facing one-pager for MTOTA booth
description: Need a one-page handout for the MTOTA symposium booth (MKTG-22) framed for OTs, not participants. Frank Schultz to review copy for professional accuracy before print. Emphasize occupation-centered pacing, not just symptom tracking.

summary: Accessibility: high-contrast and reduced-motion mode for pacing ring
description: Frank Schultz flagged that the animated ring (see cohort action item) is hard to read for low-vision clients and triggers discomfort for motion-sensitive users. Add a high-contrast static mode honoring the OS reduce-motion setting.
